CheckTitle: Third-party vendors used by the AI agent must be security-assessed (SOC 2, ISO 27001)
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Compliance**. Verifies that all third-party vendors (LLM API providers, tool integrations, data processors) used by each AI agent have been formally security-assessed, with valid SOC 2 Type II reports, ISO 27001 certificates, or equivalent assurance documentation on file.
Risk: Third-party vendors with inadequate security controls can compromise the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data processed by the agent. Supply-chain attacks against AI service providers are increasingly common. Without vendor assessments, organisations cannot make informed risk decisions about the third-party dependencies their agents rely on.

Code.Other:
1. Maintain an inventory of all third-party vendors used by each AI agent.
2. For each vendor, obtain and review current SOC 2 Type II reports, ISO 27001 certificates, or equivalent assurance documentation.
3. Assess vendor security posture against your organisation's third-party risk criteria.
4. Document assessment results, findings, and accepted residual risks.
5. Establish contractual security requirements (DPA, security addendum) with each vendor.
6. Schedule annual re-assessment and monitor for vendor security incidents.

Recommendation.Text: Maintain an assessed vendor inventory for each AI agent. Obtain SOC 2 Type II or ISO 27001 assurance for all significant third-party dependencies and conduct annual re-assessments.

Notes: ASPM-094 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
